name: pb_weekly_floatflow_smooth_20
mispricing_source: Valuation anchoring plus underreaction to smooth float-normalized flow; this is a sponsorship-path signal, not a high-correlation clone of momentum or reversal.
forced_counterparty: Retail theme chasers, benchmark-constrained growth funds, and redemption-driven sellers trading for liquidity or narrative exposure rather than long-horizon bottleneck value.
data_requirements: ["current signal test uses pb, amount, circ_market_cap", "before promotion require beta-hedged sleeve return vs 000300.SH and 399006.SZ", "rolling corr vs promoted sleeves", "residual risk attribution", "filings/backlog/capex/original-source evidence for any supplier or order claim"]
failure_mode: Fails in speculative melt-up or forced-deleveraging regimes where expensive beta leads regardless of valuation, or when low-pb plus smooth float flow is just illiquidity/distress rather than accumulation.
supply_chain_hypothesis: Candidate signal for CN power-grid, electrical equipment, and datacenter power-enabler names where float-adjusted weekly sponsorship builds before verified AI-load expansion, backlog, or order evidence is consensus.

name: low_pb_even_turnover_20
mispricing_source: Cheap AI-infra stocks with broad, non-spiky participation are typically being transferred from impatient headline-chasers to slower institutional holders before earnings/order evidence catches up.
forced_counterparty: Retail spike-chasers and benchmarked managers rotating toward already-crowded AI leaders
data_requirements: ["daily pb","daily turnover_rate","validated AI-infra universe membership","portfolio beta-hedge and residual-risk attribution ledger for sleeve deployment"]
failure_mode: It stops working when CN enters a pure speculative regime where turnover spikes, not quiet accumulation, are rewarded, or when low-PB names are value traps because backlog/capex quality is deteriorating.
supply_chain_hypothesis: Cross-layer AI-infra bottleneck names in the validated CN universe should show quiet ownership accumulation before the market fully prices domestic substitution or capacity tightness.

name: amount_stable_leader_pullback
mispricing_source: Short-horizon traders and theme money react to recent weakness, while slower institutional buyers in AI-infra leaders keep turnover value steady; the market underprices the persistence of that sponsorship. This should be less correlated to existing pure price reversals because the amount-stability gate tries to isolate sponsored leaders instead of broad losers.
forced_counterparty: Retail momentum traders, short-horizon thematic funds, and risk-budgeted accounts forced to cut recent 5-day underperformers despite intact 20-day leadership
data_requirements: ["daily amount","ret_5d","ret_20d","ai_infra universe membership and source-review status","beta-hedged sleeve return","residual risk attribution","correlation to promoted sleeves"]
failure_mode: Fails when the market flips into indiscriminate mean reversion, or when stable amount merely identifies crowded large caps with no fresh marginal buyer
supply_chain_hypothesis: none

name: low_pb_relvol_lull_20
mispricing_source: Retail/thematic capital overpays for attention and underprices temporarily quiet AI-infra names even when strategic demand exposure is unchanged.
forced_counterparty: Short-horizon theme traders and benchmark-aware managers who avoid low-attention names until volume confirms.
data_requirements: ["existing CN daily price/volume/daily_basic", "portfolio beta-hedge attribution vs 000300.SH and 399006.SZ", "sleeve correlation to promoted factors before promotion"]
failure_mode: It stops working in pure momentum melt-up regimes where attention itself is the scarce asset, or when low PB is a genuine balance-sheet/value-trap signal rather than temporary neglect.
supply_chain_hypothesis: Candidate domestic AI capex bottleneck suppliers in equipment/materials can show quiet re-accumulation after speculative volume washes out, before the next order/backlog repricing wave.

name: pb_float_absorption_20
mispricing_source: AI buildout beneficiaries in power/datacenter/heavy infrastructure get re-rated slowly because accounting value looks dull and order visibility is lagged, so steady float-scaled trading can reveal informed accumulation before the narrative catches up.
forced_counterparty: Short-horizon retail chasers and benchmarked managers rotating toward recent headline winners instead of quietly accumulating low-multiple infrastructure enablers.
data_requirements: ["daily OHLCV","daily_basic amount,pb,circ_market_cap","beta-hedge and residual-risk attribution vs 000300.SH and 399006.SZ before promotion"]
failure_mode: It breaks when low PB is a genuine balance-sheet trap or when policy/liquidity waves lift high-beta stories indiscriminately, making stable flow reflect distribution or dead money rather than accumulation.
supply_chain_hypothesis: Should preferentially surface AI datacenter power, cooling, electrical equipment, and other heavy-asset buildout names where institutions build exposure before verified order/backlog evidence is visible.

name: low_pb_float_corridor_20
mispricing_source: Quiet float-flow absorption in cheap AI-infra names is slow to clear because the market overreacts to headline beta and turnover spikes, not persistent base-building.
forced_counterparty: retail momentum traders and benchmark-constrained managers avoiding low-PB laggards
data_requirements: ["daily amount","circ_market_cap","pb","source-reviewed AI-infra universe membership","beta-hedge attribution vs 000300.SH/399006.SZ before promotion"]
failure_mode: Fails when speculative squeeze regimes reward high-turnover concept names, or when low PB reflects genuine balance-sheet deterioration instead of temporary neglect.
supply_chain_hypothesis: Candidate AI datacenter power/cooling/grid and other capex-heavy bottleneck names should show a steady float-flow corridor before verified order/backlog evidence becomes obvious.
